questions.question: Explain constructor overloading in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Constructor overloading is defining multiple constructors with same name but different parameters
Compiler differentiates based on number/type/order of parameters
Example showing default and parameterized constructors

questions.question: What is a copy constructor? When is it invoked?
keyPoints: Copy constructor initializes object using another object of same class
Signature: ClassName(const ClassName&)
Invoked when: passing by value, returning by value, initializing with another object

questions.question: Explain the difference between shallow copy and deep copy.
keyPoints: Shallow copy copies pointer addresses - multiple objects share same memory
Deep copy allocates new memory and copies actual data
Shallow copy dangerous with dynamic memory - leads to dangling pointers

questions.question: What is constructor delegation in C++11? Give syntax.
keyPoints: Delegating constructors allow one constructor to call another of same class
Syntax: Constructor1() : Constructor2(args) { }
Avoids code duplication in multiple constructors

questions.question: Why should copy constructor parameter be passed by reference?
keyPoints: Pass by value would call copy constructor recursively causing infinite loop
Reference avoids creating a copy of the argument
Should be const to prevent modification of source object

questions.question: What is the purpose of initialization list? When is it mandatory?
keyPoints: Initialization list initializes members before constructor body executes
Mandatory for const members and reference members
Also used for initializing base classes

questions.question: What is an explicit constructor? Why is it used?
keyPoints: explicit keyword prevents implicit conversions
Prevents accidental type conversions during object creation
Used to avoid unintended constructor calls

questions.question: Define a class 'Complex' with real and imaginary parts. Implement constructor overloading with: (i) Default constructor (ii) Parameterized constructor (iii) Copy constructor. Write a main function to demonstrate all three.
keyPoints: Class definition with private real, imag members
Default constructor initializes to 0,0
Parameterized constructor takes two arguments
Copy constructor copies real and imag values
Main demonstrates object creation using all three constructors

questions.question: Explain what happens when a class contains pointer members. Why is default copy constructor insufficient? Write a copy constructor that performs deep copy.
keyPoints: Default copy constructor performs shallow copy - copies pointer address
Problem: both objects point to same memory, destructor causes double-free
Deep copy constructor allocates new memory and copies data
Code showing proper deep copy implementation

questions.question: Write a C++ program to implement a String class with constructor overloading. Include: (i) Default constructor (ii) Constructor taking const char* (iii) Constructor taking another String object (copy constructor). Demonstrate proper memory management.
keyPoints: String class with char* data member
Default constructor allocates and initializes to empty string
Parameter constructor copies C-string
Copy constructor performs deep copy
Destructor frees memory properly

questions.question: What are the rules for using initialization lists in C++ constructors? Explain with examples why initialization list is necessary in certain cases.
keyPoints: Members initialized in declaration order, not list order
const members must be initialized in list
Reference members must be initialized in list
Base class constructors called via initialization list
Example showing compilation error without initialization list
